name: Proud and Ancient
description: Define your house's sigil and motto. When either is dramatically relevant to what you're attempting, test fate with favor. In a Challenge, you may spend a Resolve and Speak Incantations to cry your motto — you and any house members who hear it gain favor on their next action.

name: Read the Past
description: High elves have perfect recall. Bid lore to accurately remember anything you have ever seen or heard, no matter how long ago or how small the detail.

name: Keen Senses
description: Bid lore to sharpen one sense beyond mortal limits for a moment and ask the GM a question it could answer (read lips at distance, taste for poison, track by scent, hear the invisible). If the GM has nothing new to tell you, the bid isn't spent.

name: Foretell
description: Bid lore to ask the GM "If I do X, will Y happen?" You receive a truthful yes-or-no as a prophetic hunch.

name: Weird, Wise, Ancient
description: Whenever you bid lore, you may ask one follow-up question about the answer for free.

name: Labor Unending
description: Mark the Stressed condition to work through the night: you perform two Camp Actions this camp instead of one.

name: Hale and Hearty
description: If you eat two rations instead of one when the guild breaks bread at camp, you may charge one of your uncharged Bonds.

name: Giant's Strength
description: You automatically win contests of raw strength — arm wrestling, lifting, shoving — against anyone who isn't also a troll.

name: Quicksilver Blood
description: Your blood runs like quicksilver and eats metal. When this talent is Wounded by a metal weapon, that weapon is Destroyed.

name: Berserkergang
description: Your blood boils. When this talent is Wounded you may enter a rage: you cannot Avoid, Dodge, Speak Incantations, Banter, or Aid Another, but every Attack you make has favor. The rage ends when no enemy stands (allies who hurt you count as enemies) — or spend a Resolve to end it early.

name: Poison Blood
description: Your black blood is lethal venom. When this talent is Wounded by a bite, the creature that bit you suffers Critical damage.

name: Blur
description: Your blood rises as thick mist. When this talent is Wounded you become Shrouded — long enough to take one action, or indefinitely while you stay still.

name: Byname
description: You earn a byname — "the Tall", "the Blackhearted", "the Patron Saint of Pilgrims". When it's relevant to a test of fate, treat it like a motif and test with favor.

name: Akashic Consciousness
description: Cast your memory down your bloodline. Spend a Resolve to ask the GM any question about a past event — if any ancestor or past incarnation could plausibly have known it, the GM answers.

name: Area Sense
description: Spend 2 Resolve and meditate for a watch. Draw a card: the GM describes that many sensory impressions of your surroundings — dice clattering, a goblin's stink, the tension of a hidden trap — working outward from where you sit.

name: Spout Doom
description: Spend a Resolve to ask the GM "If I do X, what will happen?" The answer pours from your mouth unbidden, as prophecy.

name: Uncanny Knowledge
description: If nobody at the table has a motif that fits the subject at hand, you may bid lore about it anyway — whatever others don't know, you do.

name: Iron Beards
description: Spend a Resolve to automatically succeed on a test of fate that turns on strength, endurance, or sheer stubbornness.

name: Underfoot
description: Spend a Resolve to automatically succeed on a test of fate that turns on quietness or subtlety.

name: Colossal
description: Spend a Resolve to perform a feat of impossible strength that reshapes the scene — tear up a portcullis, smash through a wall, topple a column. It can't deal damage directly, but it can change a battlefield.

name: Jarl
description: Gather a goblin horde (a City Action): spend any amount of XP to attract 2 + X goblins, up to 8. The horde counts as one animal companion, obeys one-word commands in Challenges, feeds and equips itself (badly), and carries one slot of gear per goblin. Each Wound the horde takes kills one goblin.

name: Aegis
description: While you carry an intact shield, whenever a physical source (an attack, a trap) would inflict an effect on you, you may Notch your shield instead of suffering it.

name: Doom Eye
description: With a missile weapon in a Challenge, you may spend a Swords card on your turn to make a perfect shot: the Attack hits automatically, regardless of the target's Initiative.

name: Heavy Metal Machine
description: In iron or steel armor, once per round you may discard any card as a miscellaneous action to add your Swords to your Initiative as an interrupt against a single action.

name: Monster Hunter
description: Choose a hated foe and a specialization (beast, elemental, man, spirit, undead, or witch — and a specific kind of it). You gain a "[Foe] Hunter" motif for lore and tests, and your Attacks against your specialization have favor. You may change your quarry as a City Action.

name: Reaver
description: When you Attack during a Challenge, you may also move one zone as part of the charge.

name: Two-handed Focus
description: Wielding a melee weapon in both hands during a Challenge, you may Attack as a minor action with either a Pentacles or a Swords card.

name: War Stories
description: As a Camp Action, recount one of your past adventures. You and everyone who listens and plays along choose one: charge an uncharged Bond, or gain an extra point of Resolve (this may raise Resolve to 5).

name: Acrobat
description: You climb sheer surfaces and cross narrow ledges without testing fate, as easily as walking — in Challenges this movement is a miscellaneous action. Treat prepared falls as 20 feet shorter; spend a Resolve to do the same when caught off guard.

name: Ambusher
description: In the first round of a Challenge, whenever you would deal damage you deal 2 Wounds instead of 1. Spend a Resolve to shake off an ambush against you and raise the alarm for your guild.

name: Con Artist
description: After a few minutes observing or talking with someone, you may bid lore to have the GM reveal one of their likes or dislikes — your choice.

name: Fight Dirty
description: You may Roughhouse as a minor action with either a Pentacles or a Swords card, and you gain extra Roughhouse options: Exhaust, Notch, and Silence.

name: Quick!
description: While wearing light or no armor, you may treat your Pentacles actions in Challenges as interrupts.

name: Sneak
description: Declare that you slip away and go dramatically off-stage. Later, spend a Resolve to appear anywhere you could plausibly have snuck — if a Challenge hasn't started, your arrival counts as an ambush. You also gain the Infiltrate Camp Action: scout a known location, then bid lore to ask yes/no questions about it.

name: Up My Sleeve
description: Twice per crawl (once per sleeve), spend a Resolve to declare you had a common one-slot item with you the whole time — a lockpick, a dagger, a vial, a length of wire, anything the GM finds plausible.

name: Alchemy
description: You gain the Brew Alchemy Camp Action: with an alchemy kit, convert reagents harvested from Underworld monsters into potions, oils, and bombs, stored in hermetic bottles.

name: Beast Master
description: Train and command animal companions. As a Camp Action, teach one animal a command (Sic 'Em, Hunt, Guard, Fetch, Track…); trained animals know up to three. One animal becomes your familiar: it learns five commands, and when a Wound would kill it you may spend a Resolve to leave it at Death's Door instead.

name: Bookworm
description: You remember everything you read. After using the Read a Book Camp Action, you may thereafter bid lore about that text's subject. Books you've read work like motifs for lore bids (but not for tests of fate).

name: Chirurgeon
description: Expend a poultice to Heal someone (this cannot clear Stressed). You also gain the Chirurgery Camp Action: treat a non-Stressed guild-mate who is resting and Heal all of their Wounds.

name: Counsel
description: Once per round in a Challenge, shout advice to another adventurer and hand them a card from your hand. The suit must match the action you counsel; they may play it only to follow your advice. Spend a Resolve and they may play it as an interrupt.

name: High Chant
description: You gain the Perform the High Chant Camp Action: declaim epic verse, then pull cards from the discard pile equal to your Cups and hand them out (keep at most one). Each is an inspiration card — playable as a Challenge action or in place of a draw on a test of fate. One per player; they expire at session's end.

name: Loremaster
description: You are fluent in passing measure with every spoken tongue you meet, you can translate ancient scripts (short passages in a watch or two; whole books as a City Action), and you may spend a Resolve in place of a lore bid.

name: Counter-spell
description: When you perceive a sorcerer casting, spend a Resolve to Speak Incantations as an interrupt: beat their total and the spell fizzles; tie or lose and it still fizzles but you draw on the maleficence table. You may also spend a Resolve to end an ongoing spell.

name: Dwimmercraft
description: Work small magics: levitate a hand-sized object in your zone, conjure showy harmless illusions, or fake a handheld object that fools the eye until touched. Spend a Resolve to gain second sight for a watch — see the invisible and Shrouded, pierce illusions, sense enchantment and fellow sorcerers. In Challenges these are miscellaneous actions.

name: Gramarye
description: Channel the far realms through an archwood wand: blast a target up to a zone away as a weapon (Attacks with the wand count as Wands actions). You also gain the Make a Pact Camp Action: bind yourself to ascetic pacts (forego armor, forego weapons, silence, verity…) to charge spell components that substitute for Resolve when casting. Breaking a pact means drawing on the maleficence table.

name: Magic of the Wastes
description: You may cast spells of the Wastes — the grim realm of death and decay. Its spells traffic in undeath, fear, and harm. (Individual spells are listed in the rulebook's sorcery appendix.)

name: Magic of the Weald
description: You may cast spells of the Weald — the verdant, wild realm. Its spells command the elements and beasts and reshape the living world. (Individual spells are listed in the rulebook's sorcery appendix.)

name: Magic of the Weird
description: You may cast spells of the Weird — the dreaming realm untouched by sanity. Its spells bend minds, weave illusion, and warp reality. (Individual spells are listed in the rulebook's sorcery appendix.)

name: Magic of the Welkin
description: You may cast spells of the Welkin — the bright and holy realm. Its spells call on angels, heal, protect, and inspire awe. (Individual spells are listed in the rulebook's sorcery appendix.)
